sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current objects:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] not in ('B', 'G'):
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check if purple blocks changed
purple_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'p']
print(f"\nPurple blocks: {len(purple_nodes)}")
for n in purple_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check green blocks
green_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks: {len(green_nodes)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Purple blocks at the top are collectibles

- The goal might be to collect all purple blocks and then do something else

Actually, looking at the HUD bar more carefully: it was all white (64 pixels) before, and now it has 1 purple pixel at col 0 and 63 white pixels. This suggests each click on a purple block adds a purple pixel to the HUD bar.
